name: Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12
description: The gold standard of business laptops. The ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12 combines Intel Core Ultra 7 165U performance with unmatched durability — MIL-SPEC tested across 12 categories — in a carbon-fibre chassis weighing just 1.12 kg. Legendary ThinkPad keyboard, 14-inch 2.8K OLED display, and enterprise-grade security features make this the definitive laptop for business professionals.

specifications.value: Intel Core Ultra 7 165U (12-core, up to 4.9 GHz, Intel Arc GPU)
14-inch 2.8K OLED, 2880 × 1800 px, 90 Hz, 0.2 ms, 400 nits, 100% DCI-P3, Dolby Vision
57 Wh, up to 15 hours; Rapid Charge (80% in ~1 hour)
2× Thunderbolt 4, 2× USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, HDMI 2.1, 3.5 mm audio, nano-SIM slot
Fingerprint reader, IR camera (Windows Hello), ThinkShutter webcam cover, dTPM 2.0, Kensington lock slot
ThinkPad backlit keyboard with TrackPoint pointing stick
MIL-STD-810H (12 categories: dust, temperature, humidity, shock, vibration, altitude, etc.)

additionalInfo.value: Lenovo Group Ltd., No. 8 Tuo Zhong Road, Daxing District, Beijing 100176, China
ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12, 65W USB-C power adapter, documentation

reviews.title: The best business laptop ever made
reviews.reviewText: Fifteen years of ThinkPad ownership and this is the best one. The OLED display is a dramatic upgrade, the keyboard is still the finest on any laptop, and at 1.12 kg I barely notice it in my bag. The 3-year on-site warranty gives me complete confidence.

reviews.title: Built like a tank, light as a feather
reviews.reviewText: The carbon-fibre chassis flexes precisely zero. I fly 3–4 times a month and this laptop has survived overhead bins, turbulence, and a coffee spill that would have killed any other machine. Performance and battery life are equally impressive.

reviews.title: Exceptional build and keyboard, middling iGPU
reviews.reviewText: The Core Ultra 7's Arc iGPU is adequate for everyday tasks and light creative work, but if you need heavy GPU workloads you'll want a discrete GPU model. For business productivity it's faultless — the keyboard, display, and durability are simply unmatched.

reviews.title: The keyboard alone justifies the price
reviews.reviewText: The ThinkPad keyboard is a religious experience. I type faster, more accurately, and with less fatigue than on any other laptop or even most desktop keyboards. Combined with a gorgeous OLED screen and sub-1.2 kg weight, this is my perfect laptop.

reviews.title: Survived everything I've thrown at it
reviews.reviewText: Dropped it once from a desk, carry it through rain regularly, and it runs in freezing cold server rooms. Not a scratch, not a hiccup. The MIL-SPEC testing is not marketing — this laptop genuinely shrugs off conditions that destroy other laptops.
